Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.","acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-loop` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"bead":{"id":"ddx-b44ca394","title":"Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:211:{"id":"corpus-e3d55d44b044","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"atomic","rationale":"closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Runner, WorkerRecord.Route-target, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, runner, route-target, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | RUNNER | ROUTE-TARGET | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, RUNNER, ROUTE-TARGET, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. Server-submitted workers show state from WorkerManager. Local --local workers are detected via manifest scan of .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories. --json flag emits raw JSON. --watch re-renders every 2s. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. ./library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl:259:{"id":"corpus-ef0f9a2702f5","source_repo":"ddx","expected_class":"decomposable","rationale":"no_changes/review text says bead needs splitting","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Runner: claude / route-target: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of route-target time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the runner sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple runneres (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one upstream-service's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The runner should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The runner should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as runner-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the runner records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the runner catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the runner logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. data.text: This looks like a single feature slice, not a decomposition candidate: the writer already has `prompt_sha`, but the manifest schema and GraphQL efficacy path likely lag behind. I’m now reading the exact writer, the efficacy resolver, and the existing prompt-size test to connect each AC to a concrete code path before touching anything.

type PersonaDeleteResult {
  """True when the persona was deleted"""
  ok: Boolean!
  """Persona name that was deleted"""
  name: String!
}

"""EfficacyWarning flags a model row that needs operator attention."""
type EfficacyWarning {
  """Warning kind"""
  kind: String!
  """Numeric threshold involved in the warning"""
  threshold: Float
}

"""EfficacyRow summarizes agent performance for one harness/provider/model tuple."""
type EfficacyRow {
  """Stable row key"""
  rowKey: String!
  """Agent harness"""
  harness: String!
  """Provider name"""
  provider: String!
  """Model identifier"""
  model: String!
  """Attempt count"""
  attempts: Int!
  """Successful attempt count"""
  successes: Int!
  """Successes divided by attempts"""
  successRate: Float!
  """Median input tokens"""
  medianInputTokens: Int!
  """Median output tokens"""
  medianOutputTokens: Int!
  """Median duration in milliseconds"""
  medianDurationMs: Int!
  """Median cost in USD when known"""
  medianCostUsd: Float
  """Hourly success-rate sparkline for the last 24 hours, oldest-first"""
  sparkline: [Int!]
  """Optional warning metadata"""
  warning: EfficacyWarning
}

"""EfficacyAttempt is one historical attempt for an efficacy row."""
type EfficacyAttempt {
  """Bead identifier"""
  beadId: String!
  """Attempt outcome"""
  outcome: String!
  """Duration in milliseconds"""
  durationMs: Int!
  """Cost in USD when known"""
  costUsd: Float
  """Execution evidence URL or path"""
  evidenceBundleUrl: String!
}

"""EfficacyAttempts groups attempt details for a row."""
type EfficacyAttempts {
  """Stable row key"""
  rowKey: String!
  """Attempts in newest-first order"""
  attempts: [EfficacyAttempt!]!
}

"""ComparisonRecord is a queued or completed model comparison."""
type ComparisonRecord {
  """Comparison identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Comparison state"""
  state: String!
  """Number of configured arms"""
  armCount: Int!
}

data.text: I confirmed the efficacy view currently groups only by `harness/provider/model`, so the new slice-by-prompt requirement will need a code change there, not just schema drift. I’m locating the manifest write path and the GraphQL schema surface next so I can change both consistently and keep the generated layer in sync.

data.output:
# DDx CLI Makefile

# Build variables
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty)
COMMIT ?= $(shell git rev-parse --short HEAD)
DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
LDFLAGS = -ldflags "-X main.version=${VERSION} -X main.commit=${COMMIT} -X main.date=${DATE}"

# Build directory
BUILD_DIR = build

# Platforms to build for
PLATFORMS = \
	darwin/amd64 \
	darwin/arm64 \
	linux/amd64 \
	linux/arm64 \
	windows/amd64

.PHONY: all build clean test lint deps help obsidian-migrate obsidian-validate obsidian-test obsidian-dry-run obsidian-nav obsidian-revert bead-schema skill-schema gendocs frontend-build frontend-rebuild frontend-verify copy-skills eval-skill bench-graphql

# Default target
all: clean deps test build

# Build SvelteKit frontend (required before Go build for //go:embed).
# Skip rebuild when no source file is newer than the last build output.
FRONTEND_SRC := $(shell find internal/server/frontend/src internal/server/frontend/static -type f 2>/dev/null)
FRONTEND_STAMP := internal/server/frontend/build/index.html

frontend-build: $(FRONTEND_STAMP)

$(FRONTEND_STAMP): $(FRONTEND_SRC) internal/server/frontend/package.json internal/server/frontend/svelte.config.js internal/server/frontend/vite.config.ts
	@echo "Building SvelteKit frontend..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Force a frontend rebuild regardless of timestamps
frontend-rebuild:
	@echo "Rebuilding SvelteKit frontend (forced)..."
	cd internal/server/frontend && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory frontend-verify

# Verify the embedded frontend bundle is internally consistent: every
# /_app/* asset referenced by index.html must exist on disk. Catches the
# class of bug where index.html and the chunk dir get out of sync (partial
# build, file restore, manual edit) and go:embed silently bakes in a
# broken bundle that 404s every JS chunk to the SPA shell.
frontend-verify:
	@echo "Verifying embedded frontend bundle integrity..."
	@cd internal/server/frontend/build && \
	missing=0; \
	for ref in $$(grep -hoE '"/_app/[^"]+"' index.html | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do \
		path=".$$ref"; \
		if [ ! -f "$$path" ]; then \
			echo "  MISSING: $$ref"; \
			missing=$$((missing+1)); \
		fi; \
	done; \
	if [ $$missing -gt 0 ]; then \
		echo "frontend-verify: $$missing referenced asset(s) missing — run 'make frontend-rebuild'"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi; \
	echo "  ok"

# Copy the canonical top-level skills/ddx/ tree into cli/internal/skills/ddx/
# so //go:embed (which cannot traverse upward out of the package) picks it up.
# Runs as a prereq of build/test/dev so contributors never see stale embedded
# skills. rsync --delete keeps the destination in exact sync.
SKILLS_SRC := ../skills/ddx
SKILLS_DST := internal/skills/ddx
copy-skills:
	@if [ ! -d "$(SKILLS_SRC)" ]; then \
		echo "error: $(SKILLS_SRC) not found (run from cli/)"; exit 1; \
	fi
	@rsync -a --delete $(SKILLS_SRC)/ $(SKILLS_DST)/

# Build for current platform
build: copy-skills frontend-build
	@echo "Building DDx CLI..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .

# Build for all platforms
build-all: clean deps copy-skills
	@echo "Building for all platforms..."
	@mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
	@echo "Building current platform..."
	go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx .
	@for platform in $(PLATFORMS); do \
		OS=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f1); \
		ARCH=$$(echo $$platform | cut -d'/' -f2); \
		echo "Building $$OS/$$ARCH..."; \
		GOOS=$$OS GOARCH=$$ARCH go build $(LDFLAGS) -o $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH .; \
		if [ $$OS = "windows" ]; then \
			mv $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx-$$OS-$$ARCH.exe; \
		fi; \
	done

# Install dependencies
deps:
	@echo "Installing dependencies..."
	go mod download
	go mod tidy

# Run tests
test: copy-skills
	@echo "Running tests..."
	go test -v ./...

# Run the GraphQL perf harness (ddx-9ce6842a). Seeds a 10 × 500 bead fixture
# and records baseline numbers under docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/. Override
# the fixture size or the report date via BENCH_REPORT_DATE.
BENCH_REPORT_DIR ?= $(abspath ../docs/helix/06-iterate/perf)
BENCH_REPORT_DATE ?= $(shell date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
bench-graphql:
	@echo "Running GraphQL perf harness (fixture: 10 × 500 beads = 5,000)..."
	@mkdir -p $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR=$(BENCH_REPORT_DIR) \
	DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DATE=$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE) \
	go test ./internal/server/perf/ -run TestGraphQLPerfMatrix_Baseline -v -timeout 5m
	@echo "Baseline report written to $(BENCH_REPORT_DIR)/$(BENCH_REPORT_DATE)-baseline.md"

# Validate the shared bead record schema
bead-schema:
	@echo "Validating bead record schema..."
	go test ./internal/bead -run 'TestBeadRecordSchema' -count=1

# Validate bundled skill metadata
skill-schema:
	@echo "Validating bundled skill metadata..."
	go run . skills check ../skills internal/skills

# Run linter
lint:
	@echo "Running linter..."
	@if command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		golangci-lint run; \
	else \
		echo "golangci-lint not found, running go vet instead"; \
		go vet ./...; \
	fi
	@echo "Running routinglint (FEAT-006 anti-reintroduction gate)..."
	@go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...

# Install locally for development
install: build
	@echo "Installing DDx locally..."
	cp $(BUILD_DIR)/ddx $(HOME)/.local/bin/ddx
	@echo "DDx installed to ~/.local/bin/ddx"

# Clean build artifacts
clean:
	@echo "Cleaning..."
	rm -rf $(BUILD_DIR)

# Run the CLI (for development)
run:
	go run . $(ARGS)

# Format code
fmt:
	go fmt ./...

# Update dependencies
update-deps:
	go get -u all
	go mod tidy

# Create release archives
release: build-all
	@echo "Creating release archives..."
	@cd $(BUILD_DIR) && \
	for file in ddx-*; do \
		if [[ $$file == *.exe ]]; then \
			zip $${file%.*}.zip $$file; \
		else \
			tar -czf $$file.tar.gz $$file; \
		fi; \
	done

# Development server (watches for changes)
dev: copy-skills
	@echo "Starting development mode..."
	@if command -v air >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		air; \
	else \
		echo "Air not found. Install with: go install github.com/cosmtrek/air@latest"; \
		echo "Falling back to basic run..."; \
		go run . $(ARGS); \
	fi

# Obsidian integration targets
obsidian-migrate: build
	@echo "Migrating HELIX workflow to Obsidian format..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate workflows/helix

obsidian-validate: build
	@echo "Validating Obsidian format in HELIX workflow..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian validate workflows/helix

obsidian-test:
	@echo "Running Obsidian integration tests..."
	go test ./internal/obsidian/... -v
	go test ./internal/obsidian/converter/... -v
	go test ./internal/obsidian/validator/... -v

obsidian-dry-run: build
	@echo "Running Obsidian migration dry-run..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian migrate --dry-run workflows/helix

obsidian-nav: build
	@echo "Generating navigation hub..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian nav workflows/helix

obsidian-revert: build
	@echo "Reverting Obsidian migration..."
	./$(BUILD_DIR)/ddx obsidian revert --strip-frontmatter workflows/helix

# Generate Hugo markdown docs for every CLI command
gendocs:

data.text: The GraphQL layer is gqlgen-backed, so I’m checking whether the repo expects regeneration or a checked-in manual update path. If there’s a generator target, I’ll use that; otherwise I’ll patch the minimal generated surface that the tests exercise.

data.output:
# Location of all your GraphQL files (relative to this config file's directory)
schema:
  - schema.graphql

# Location where the generated server code will be written
exec:
  filename: generated.go
  package: graphql

# Configuration for resolvers generated by gqlgen
resolver:
  filename: resolver.go
  package: graphql
  type: Resolver

# Location of the models
model:
  filename: models.go

# DDx GraphQL Schema
# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.

# ============================================
# Scalars
# ============================================

"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
scalar DateTime

"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
scalar JSON

# ============================================
# Interfaces
# ============================================

"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
interface Node {
  """Globally unique identifier"""
  id: ID!
}

# ============================================
# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
# ============================================

"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
type PageInfo {
  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
  hasNextPage: Boolean!
  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  startCursor: String
  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
  endCursor: String
}

# ============================================
# Node & Project
# ============================================

"""NodeInfo represents the running ddx-server instance"""
type NodeInfo implements Node {
  """Unique node identifier (stable across restarts)"""
  id: ID!
  """Human-readable node name"""
  name: String!
  """Timestamp when the server process started"""
  startedAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
}

"""Project represents a registered ddx project on this node"""
type Project implements Node {
  """Unique project identifier"""
  id: ID!
  """Short display name"""
  name: String!
  """Absolute filesystem path to the project root"""
  path: String!
  """Git remote URL, if configured"""
  gitRemote: String
  """When the project was first registered with the server"""
  registeredAt: DateTime!
  """Timestamp of the last successful probe"""
  lastSeen: DateTime!
  """True when the project path is inaccessible"""
  unreachable: Boolean
  """Set when the project has been tombstoned (soft-deleted)"""
  tombstonedAt: DateTime
}

"""ProjectEdge is one edge in a ProjectConnection"""
type ProjectEdge {
  """The project at this position"""
  node: Project!
  """Opaque cursor for pagination"""
  cursor: String!
}

"""ProjectConnection is a Relay cursor connection of projects"""
type ProjectConnection {
  """Ordered list of project edges"""
  edges: [ProjectEdge!]!
  """Pagination metadata"""
  pageInfo: PageInfo!
  """Total number of projects matching the filter"""
  totalCount: Int!
}

# ============================================
# Beads
# ============================================

"""Bead is a portable work item with lifecycle metadata"""
type Bead implements Node {
  """Unique bead identifier (e.g. "ddx-a1b2c3d4")"""
  id: ID!
  """Short summary of the work"""
  title: String!
  """Lifecycle status: open, in-progress, closed, blocked"""
  status: String!
  """Numeric priority (lower is higher priority)"""
  priority: Int!
  """Issue classification (e.g. "task", "bug", "spike")"""
  issueType: String!
  """Agent or human currently responsible"""
  owner: String
  """When the bead was created"""
  createdAt: DateTime!
  """Who or what created the bead"""
  createdBy: String
  """Last modification timestamp"""
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  """Freeform labels for filtering and grouping"""
  labels: [String!]

  id: TD-028
  depends_on:
    - FEAT-022
  status: draft
  implementation: deferred

# Technical Design: Prompt Versioning, `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` Selector, and Offline Comparison

## Status

Design only. **Implementation is deferred.** This TD captures the
measurement roadmap that grew out of Story 12 (optimize the static
prompts that drive `ddx agent execute-bead`). Story 12 lands the
mechanical pieces — guardrail dedup (B1/B2) and `prompt_sha` on the
attempt manifest (B3). Everything below — the human-readable
`prompt_version` field, the `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` environment selector
for offline A/B, and the offline-comparison roadmap that consumes
them — is intentionally NOT implemented in Story 12. A follow-up
story will pick this up once we have enough post-Story-12 attempts
in `.ddx/executions/` to know which slices actually move the cheap-
tier metrics.

## Motivation

The Story 12 rewrite tightens two ~1,000-word static prompts
(`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText`, `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`
in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`) by ~30% and adds a
load-bearing-guardrail comment contract (FEAT-022 amendment). Two
risks fall out of that work:

1. **No way to tell whether the rewrite helped.** Cheap-tier behavior
   on the new shorter prompts could be better, the same, or worse, and
   we have no clean way to slice attempt outcomes (efficacy,
   `no_evidence_produced`, escalation rate) by prompt body.
2. **No way to A/B alternative wordings without shipping them.** Once
   the rewrite lands, every future tightening is a flying-blind edit
   to a constant, with the same measurement gap.

B3 closes part of (1) by writing `prompt_sha` (sha256 hex of the
rendered prompt bytes) into every attempt's `manifest.json`. That is
sufficient for a coarse before/after slice, but it has two
limitations:

- A sha is opaque. A reviewer reading an analytics dashboard cannot
  tell from `prompt_sha=ab12…` what version of the prompt produced
  the row.
- It only differentiates whatever variant DDx happened to ship at the
  time. There is no mechanism to run an experimental variant
  side-by-side with the live one.

This TD describes how we close both gaps without growing the
runtime surface area.

## Design

### 1. `prompt_version` field on the attempt manifest

`AttemptManifest` already carries `prompt_sha` (see
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` field `PromptSHA` at line 177
and `promptSHA(...)` at line 1478). Add an adjacent string field
`prompt_version` populated from a per-(harness, variant) constant
defined alongside the prompt body:

```go
const (
    // executeBeadPromptVersionClaude is bumped by hand whenever
    // executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText changes in a way that
    // analytics should treat as a new prompt. Format:
    // "<story>-<harness>-<n>", e.g. "story12-claude-1".
    executeBeadPromptVersionClaude = "story12-claude-1"
    executeBeadPromptVersionAgent  = "story12-agent-1"
)
```

Rules:

- The version string is a developer-curated label, NOT computed.
  Whitespace-only edits do not bump it; semantic edits do. The
  guardrail-list comment block above each prompt constant
  (FEAT-022 amendment) is the natural place to record the version
  bump alongside the change.
- `prompt_sha` remains the source of truth for "is this attempt
  byte-identical to that one." `prompt_version` is the human label
  used for grouping in dashboards and changelogs.
- Both fields are written into `manifest.json`. Existing readers
  that ignore unknown fields are unaffected.

Why both: a sha alone forces every analytics consumer to maintain
its own sha→label mapping. A label alone allows accidental
collisions (developer forgets to bump). Carrying both keeps the
sha as the authoritative grouping key and the label as the
display name.

### 2. `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` environment selector

Add an opt-in environment variable read at prompt-render time:

- `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` unset (default): render the live prompt as
  today. Behavior is identical to current code paths.
- `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=<name>`: select an experimental prompt body
  registered in a small in-process registry, e.g.
  `map[string]promptBundle` keyed by `<name>`, where
  `promptBundle` contains the `{claudeText, agentText, version}`
  triple.

Constraints:

- The selector ONLY swaps the static body. The harness selector
  (`agent`/`fiz`/`embedded` → Agent variant, everything else →
  Claude variant; see `execute_bead.go:1343`) is unchanged. An
  experimental bundle must supply BOTH variants.
- Unknown variant name is a hard error at render time, not a
  silent fallback. Operators running an A/B want loud failure if
  they typo the variant name.
- The variant name (and its version label) is recorded on the
  manifest as `prompt_variant` and folded into `prompt_version`
  (e.g. `experiment-tighter-step0-claude-1`). `prompt_sha` still
  hashes the actual rendered bytes, so it remains the
  authoritative grouping key.
- Variants live in source under
  `cli/internal/agent/prompt_variants/` with the same load-bearing-
  guardrail comment contract as the live prompts. A variant that
  drops a guardrail fails the FEAT-022 regression test, same as an
  edit to the live prompt would.
- Variants are NOT a config-file concept. They are compiled in.
  This keeps offline experimentation a developer activity, not an
  end-user surface, and avoids inventing a new config schema for
  something we expect to retire after each round of tuning.

Why an env var and not a CLI flag: `ddx agent execute-bead` is
invoked by the loop (`agent execute-loop` / `ddx work`), and the
loop does not currently thread per-call CLI flags into the
prompt-render path. An env var is the cheapest way to attach a
variant selection to a whole drain run without rewriting the
loop's argv plumbing.

### 3. Offline-comparison roadmap

The combination of `prompt_sha`, `prompt_version`, and
`prompt_variant` on the manifest lets us reuse the telemetry that
already exists in the repo, without standing up new infrastructure:

- **`.ddx/executions/<run-id>/manifest.json`** — the per-attempt
  record this TD extends. Already includes outcome, harness, bead
  id, base rev, and (post-B3) `prompt_sha`. Adding
  `prompt_version` and `prompt_variant` here is the only schema
  change this roadmap requires.
- **`.ddx/executions/<run-id>/usage.json`** — token usage emitted
  by harnesses that report it. Joined to the manifest by
  `<run-id>`, this gives a per-prompt-version cost profile.
- **`session_index.go`** (`cli/internal/agent/session_index.go`)
  — already maintains the session-token index that ties an
  attempt's run id to its harness session events. No change
  required; new fields ride along.
- **`resolver_feat008.go`** (`cli/internal/server/graphql/`) —
  exposes the efficacy view (`EfficacyRows`, `EfficacyAttempts`).
  The efficacy resolver already aggregates per attempt; extending
  it to group/filter by `prompt_version` or `prompt_variant` is a
  resolver-only change once the manifest fields are populated.

Concretely, the offline workflow becomes:

1. Operator sets `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT=experiment-foo` and runs
   `ddx work` over a representative bead queue (or replays a
   captured queue with `--from <rev>`).
2. Each attempt writes a manifest tagged
   `prompt_variant=experiment-foo`,
   `prompt_version=experiment-foo-claude-1`, plus the rendered
   `prompt_sha`.
3. The efficacy view (extended) groups attempts by
   `prompt_version` and reports outcome distribution, retry
   count, escalation rate, mean tokens, and mean wall time per
   group. A second drain with the variant unset or set to a
   different name produces the comparison set.
4. If the experiment wins, promote the variant: copy its body
   over the live constant, bump the live version label, and
   delete the variant entry. If it loses, delete the variant.

Out of scope for this TD:

- Statistical significance testing. The cheap-tier sample sizes
  in our queue are too small for clean significance claims;
  decisions will be directional ("variant clearly worse → drop"
  or "variant clearly better → ship"), not p-value gated.
- Online A/B (random-assigning live attempts to variants). The
  selector is offline-only by design — a single run uses a single
  variant. Online A/B would require per-attempt variant
  assignment, manifest schema changes beyond this TD, and a story
  of its own.
- Cross-project comparison. The efficacy view is per-project; a
  variant evaluated in one project's queue is not automatically
  comparable to another's.

### Interactions with existing code

- `buildPrompt` (`execute_bead.go:1392`): the natural insertion
  point for variant lookup. It already owns prompt assembly; it
  would gain a `selectVariant(harness, os.Getenv(...))` call
  before reading the static constants.
- `manifest.json` writer
  (`AttemptManifest` struct + the `PromptSHA:` write at
  `execute_bead.go:1127`): gains two adjacent string fields. Same
  serialization path.
- `executions_mirror.go`: already mirrors manifests into
  `.ddx/executions/` for the resolver to read. No change.
- `BuildReviewPromptBounded` and `beadReviewInstructions`: not
  in scope. The reviewer prompt is intentionally untouched by
  Story 12 (AC9) and remains untouched by this TD.

## Non-goals

- This TD does not propose a new config-file surface. No
  `.ddx.yml` keys, no new commands.
- This TD does not propose runtime variant rotation. One drain
  uses one variant.
- This TD does not propose changing the harness selector. The
  Claude/Agent split (`execute_bead.go:1343`) is preserved per
  Story 12 AC10.
- This TD does not propose statistical-test infrastructure.

## Migration / rollout

When a follow-up story implements this:

1. Land `prompt_version` first, defaulting to the current Story
   12 labels. Manifests gain the field; nothing else changes.
   Backfill is unnecessary — historical manifests stay on
   `prompt_sha` only, and the resolver treats missing
   `prompt_version` as a sentinel ("pre-versioning").
2. Land the `DDX_PROMPT_VARIANT` selector and the variants
   directory. Default behavior (variable unset) is unchanged.
3. Extend the efficacy resolver's grouping last. Until then,
   offline comparison is "grep manifests by `prompt_version`."

## Open questions

- Should `prompt_version` be enforced via a unit test that fails
  when the constant body changes without a corresponding version
  bump? A naive sha-of-body equality check is workable but
  noisy on whitespace edits. Defer the decision to the
  implementing story; the FEAT-022 guardrail-list comment
  contract already gives reviewers a place to catch missed
  bumps.
- Should the variant registry live in a `prompt_variants_test.go`
  build-tagged file rather than production code, so experimental
  bodies do not ship in the binary? Probably yes for variants
  intended only for one-off offline runs; defer.

cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl:1:{"id":"as-bc4b0e90","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:50:16.151938804Z","harness":"claude","surface":"claude","canonical_target":"claude-sonnet-4-6","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","prompt_len":13868,"prompt":"\u003cexecute-bead\u003e\n  \u003cbead id=\"axon-dccbdf73\"\u003e\n    \u003ctitle\u003eauth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension\u003c/title\u003e\n    \u003cdescription\u003e\nImplement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.\n\n## What you're building\n\nAn axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.\n\n## Scope — exactly what to add\n\n### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`\n\nA module exposing two things:\n\n1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec\u003cu8\u003e` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(\u0026self, claims: \u0026JwtClaims) -\u003e Result\u003cString, AuthError\u003e` and a `verify(\u0026self, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` for round-tripping in tests.\n\n2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.\n\nThe middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock\u003cHashSet\u003cUuid\u003e\u003e` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`\n\n- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.\n- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(\u0026self) -\u003e u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 §4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)\n- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(\u0026self, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026GrantedDatabase\u003e` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(\u0026self, op: Op) -\u003e bool`.\n\n### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)\n\nAdd one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:\n\n```rust\nasync fn is_jti_revoked(\u0026self, jti: Uuid) -\u003e Result\u003cbool, AxonError\u003e;\n```\n\nImplement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet\u003cUuid\u003e`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table — just query it.\n\n### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`\n\nRe-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet — that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.\n\n## Verification order (normative — ADR-018 §4)\n\n1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e` from the request. Missing header → `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead — that's E2.\n2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid → `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure → `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array → `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper — use it).\n3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past → `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future → `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).\n4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit → `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.\n5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch → `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 — see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Option\u003c(\u0026str, \u0026str)\u003e` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.\n6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cUser\u003e, AxonError\u003e` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` → `UserSuspended` (401).\n7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -\u003e Result\u003cOption\u003cTenantMember\u003e, AxonError\u003e`. Missing → `NotATenantMember` (403).\n8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None → `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found → compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection → `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead — the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.\n9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.\n\n## Error mapping — must exactly match ADR-018 §4\n\nThe mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.\n\n| Failure | Status | `error.code` |\n|---|---|---|\n| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |\n| Header not `Bearer \u003ctoken\u003e` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |\n| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |\n| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |\n| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |\n| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |\n| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` — for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |\n| `aud` ≠ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |\n| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |\n| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |\n| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |\n| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |\n\nNote the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.\n\n## Testing — all must pass for this bead to be complete\n\n### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`\n\nFor every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.\n\nAdditionally:\n\n- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.\n- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.\n- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep — invalidate the cache directly.\n- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.\n- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.\n\n### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`\n\nBuild a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:\n- No auth header → 401 `unauthenticated`\n- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: \"orders\", ops: [read]}]` → 200\n- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud → 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`\n- Valid JWT with grants for a different database → 403 `database_not_granted`\n\n### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`\n\n**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.\n\n## Crate dependencies you will need\n\nAdd to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:\n```toml\njsonwebtoken = \"9\"\n```\n\nEverything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.\n\n## Context you must know about\n\n### A1 landed: schema\n- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.\n- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) — already corrected.\n- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.\n\n### A2 landed: types\n- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).\n- `parse_claims(\u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003cJwtClaims, AuthError\u003e` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.\n- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.\n- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.\n\n### Files you should NOT touch\n- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.\n- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` — that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.\n\n## Quality bar\n\nYou are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit — the bead will be re-queued.\n\nSpecifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.\n    \u003c/description\u003e\n    \u003cacceptance\u003e\n- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles\n- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)\n- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order\n- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 §4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)\n- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep\n- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)\n- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths\n- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)\n- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly\n- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)\n- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n    \u003c/acceptance\u003e\n    \u003clabels\u003ehelix, area:auth, spec:ADR-018, phase:middleware\u003c/labels\u003e\n    \u003cmetadata parent=\"axon-b170a173\" base-rev=\"HEAD\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual\"/\u003e\n  \u003c/bead\u003e\n\n  \u003cgoverning\u003e\n    \u003cref id=\"ADR-018\" path=\"docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-018-tenant-user-credential-model.md\"\u003eSections 4 (Credentials) and 4 Verification Order — normative for everything in this bead\u003c/ref\u003e\n    \u003cref id=\"FEAT-012\" path=\"docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-012-authorization.md\"\u003eCredentials (JWT, V5) and Identity (Authentication) sections\u003c/ref\u003e\n  \u003c/governing\u003e\n\n  \u003cinstructions\u003e\nYou are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Axon server — HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\\n2\\t//!\\n3\\t//! # Authentication\\n4\\t//!\\n5\\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\\n6\\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\\n7\\t//!\\n8\\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\\n9\\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\\n10\\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\\\"anonymous\\\"` | Admin |\\n11\\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\\n12\\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role \u003crole\u003e` | `\\\"guest\\\"` | configured role |\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\\n15\\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\\n16\\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=\u003cpeer\u003e` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\\n17\\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\\n18\\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! ACL tag → role mapping:\\n21\\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` → [`Role::Admin`]\\n22\\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` → [`Role::Write`]\\n23\\t//! - `tag:axon-read` → [`Role::Read`]\\n24\\t//! - no matching tag → `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\\n25\\t//!\\n26\\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\\n27\\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\\n28\\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\\n29\\t//!\\n30\\t//! # Request flow\\n31\\t//!\\n32\\t//! ```text\\n33\\t//! TCP accept\\n34\\t//!   └─ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\\n35\\t//!        ├─ AuthContext::resolve_peer  →  Identity\\n36\\t//!        └─ insert Identity into request extensions\\n37\\t//!             └─ route handler\\n38\\t//!                  ├─ extract Extension\u003cIdentity\u003e\\n39\\t//!                  ├─ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\\n40\\t//!                  └─ handler logic\\n41\\t//! ```\\n42\\t//!\\n43\\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\\n44\\tpub mod Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\\n1\\t//!\\n2\\t//! # Layers\\n3\\t//!\\n4\\t//! **Layer 1 — RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\\n5\\t//!\\n6\\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`) control access to\\n7\\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\\n8\\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\\n9\\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\\n10\\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\\n11\\t//!\\n12\\t//! **Layer 2 — Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\\n13\\t//!\\n14\\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\\n15\\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\\n16\\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\\n17\\t//!\\n18\\t//! **Layer 3 — Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\\n19\\t//!\\n20\\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\\n21\\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\\n22\\t//!\\n23\\t//! **Layer 4 — Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\\n24\\t//!\\n25\\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\\\"*\\\"` for all databases).\\n26\\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\\n27\\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\\n28\\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\\n29\\t\\n30\\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\n31\\t\\n32\\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\\n33\\t\\n34\\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\\n35\\t///\\n36\\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin \u003e Write \u003e Read \u003e None`.\\n37\\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\\n38\\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\\n39\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n40\\tpub enum Role {\\n41\\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\\n42\\t    None = 0,\\n43\\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\\n44\\t    Read = 1,\\n45\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\\n46\\t    Write = 2,\\n47\\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\\n48\\t    Admin = 3,\\n49\\t}\\n50\\t\\n51\\timpl Role {\\n52\\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\\n53\\t    ///\\n54\\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\\n55\\t    ///\\n56\\t    /// | Input | Role |\\n57\\t    /// |-------|------|\\n58\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-admin\\\"` / `\\\"tag:admin\\\"` / `\\\"admin\\\"` | `Admin` |\\n59\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-write\\\"` / `\\\"tag:axon-agent\\\"` / `\\\"tag:write\\\"` / `\\\"write\\\"` | `Write` |\\n60\\t    /// | `\\\"tag:axon-read\\\"` / `\\\"tag:read\\\"` / `\\\"read\\\"` | `Read` |\\n61\\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\\n62\\t    ///\\n63\\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` — the conventional tag for\\n64\\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\\n65\\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: \u0026str) -\u003e Self {\\n66\\t        match tag {\\n67\\t            \\\"tag:axon-admin\\\" | \\\"tag:admin\\\" | \\\"admin\\\" =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n68\\t            \\\"tag:axon-write\\\" | \\\"tag:axon-agent\\\" | \\\"tag:write\\\" | \\\"write\\\" =\u003e Role::Write,\\n69\\t            \\\"tag:axon-read\\\" | \\\"tag:read\\\" | \\\"read\\\" =\u003e Role::Read,\\n70\\t            _ =\u003e Role::None,\\n71\\t        }\\n72\\t    }\\n73\\t\\n74\\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\\n75\\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: \u0026[String]) -\u003e Self {\\n76\\t        tags.iter()\\n77\\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\\n78\\t            .max()\\n79\\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\\n80\\t    }\\n81\\t}\\n82\\t\\n83\\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\\n84\\t    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {\\n85\\t        match self {\\n86\\t            Role::None =\u003e write!(f, \\\"none\\\"),\\n87\\t            Role::Read =\u003e write!(f, \\\"read\\\"),\\n88\\t            Role::Write =\u003e write!(f, \\\"write\\\"),\\n89\\t            Role::Admin =\u003e write!(f, \\\"admin\\\"),\\n90\\t        }\\n91\\t    }\\n92\\t}\\n93\\t\\n94\\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\\n95\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\\n96\\tpub enum Operation {\\n97\\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\\n98\\t    Read,\\n99\\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\\n100\\t    Write,\\n101\\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\\n102\\t    Admin,\\n103\\t}\\n104\\t\\n105\\timpl Operation {\\n106\\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\\n107\\t    pub fn required_role(self) -\u003e Role {\\n108\\t        match self {\\n109\\t            Operation::Read =\u003e Role::Read,\\n110\\t            Operation::Write =\u003e Role::Write,\\n111\\t            Operation::Admin =\u003e Role::Admin,\\n112\\t        }\\n113\\t    }\\n114\\t}\\n115\\t\\n116\\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\\n117\\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\\n118\\t///\\n119\\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\\n120\\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\\n121\\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\\n122\\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\\n123\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n124\\tpub struct EntityFilter {\\n125\\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\\n126\\t    pub field: String,\\n127\\t    /// The required value for that field.\\n128\\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\\n129\\t}\\n130\\t\\n131\\timpl EntityFilter {\\n132\\t    /// Convenience constructor.\\n133\\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, value: serde_json::Value) -\u003e Self {\\n134\\t        Self {\\n135\\t            field: field.into(),\\n136\\t            value,\\n137\\t        }\\n138\\t    }\\n139\\t\\n140\\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\\n141\\t    ///\\n142\\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\\n143\\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\\n144\\t    pub fn matches(\u0026self, data: \u0026serde_json::Value) -\u003e bool {\\n145\\t        data.get(\u0026self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == \u0026self.value)\\n146\\t    }\\n147\\t}\\n148\\t\\n149\\t/// Identity context for a request.\\n150\\t///\\n151\\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\\n152\\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\\n153\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n154\\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\\n155\\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\\n156\\t    pub actor: String,\\n157\\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\\n158\\t    pub role: Role,\\n159\\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\\n160\\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\\n161\\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\\n162\\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \\\"Option::is_none\\\")]\\n163\\t    pub entity_filter: Option\u003cEntityFilter\u003e,\\n164\\t}\\n165\\t\\n166\\timpl CallerIdentity {\\n167\\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\\n168\\t    pub fn anonymous() -\u003e Self {\\n169\\t        Self {\\n170\\t            actor: \\\"anonymous\\\".into(),\\n171\\t            role: Role::Admin,\\n172\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n173\\t        }\\n174\\t    }\\n175\\t\\n176\\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\\n177\\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into\u003cString\u003e, role: Role) -\u003e Self {\\n178\\t        Self {\\n179\\t            actor: actor.into(),\\n180\\t            role,\\n181\\t            entity_filter: None,\\n182\\t        }\\n183\\t    }\\n184\\t\\n185\\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\\n186\\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -\u003e Self {\\n187\\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\\n188\\t        self\\n189\\t    }\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\\n192\\t    ///\\n193\\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\\n194\\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\\n195\\t    pub fn check(\u0026self, op: Operation) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n196\\t        let required = op.required_role();\\n197\\t        if self.role \u003e= required {\\n198\\t            Ok(())\\n199\\t        } else {\\n200\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n201\\t                \\\"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\\\",\\n202\\t                self.role, op, required\\n203\\t            )))\\n204\\t        }\\n205\\t    }\\n206\\t\\n207\\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\\n208\\t    ///\\n209\\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\\n210\\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\\n211\\t    /// removed from the data.\\n212\\t    pub fn apply_masks(\u0026self, data: \u0026mut serde_json::Value, policies: \u0026[MaskPolicy]) {\\n213\\t        if self.role \u003e= Role::Admin {\\n214\\t            return; // Admin sees all.\\n215\\t        }\\n216\\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\\n217\\t            for policy in policies {\\n218\\t                if self.role \u003c policy.min_role {\\n219\\t                    obj.remove(\u0026policy.field);\\n220\\t                }\\n221\\t            }\\n222\\t        }\\n223\\t    }\\n224\\t}\\n225\\t\\n226\\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\\n227\\t///\\n228\\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\\n229\\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\\n230\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n231\\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\\n232\\t    /// The field name to mask.\\n233\\t    pub field: String,\\n234\\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\\n235\\t    pub min_role: Role,\\n236\\t}\\n237\\t\\n238\\t// ── Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ────────────────────────\\n239\\t\\n240\\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\\n241\\t///\\n242\\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\\n243\\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\\n244\\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\\n245\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\n246\\tpub struct WritePolicy {\\n247\\t    /// Collection this policy applies to.\\n248\\t    pub collection: String,\\n249\\t    /// Minimum role required for write operations.\\n250\\t    pub min_write_role: Role,\\n251\\t    /// Fields that cannot be modified after the initial create.\\n252\\t    #[serde(default)]\\n253\\t    pub immutable_fields: Vec\u003cString\u003e,\\n254\\t}\\n255\\t\\n256\\timpl WritePolicy {\\n257\\t    /// Check if a caller has sufficient role to write to this collection.\\n258\\t    pub fn check_write(\u0026self, caller: \u0026CallerIdentity) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n259\\t        if caller.role \u003e= self.min_write_role {\\n260\\t            Ok(())\\n261\\t        } else {\\n262\\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\\n263\\t                \\\"write policy for '{}': role '{}' insufficient (requires '{}')\\\",\\n264\\t                self.collection, caller.role, self.min_write_role\\n265\\t            )))\\n266\\t        }\\n267\\t    }\\n268\\t\\n269\\t    /// Check if any immutable fields have been modified.\\n270\\t    ///\\n271\\t    /// Returns the list of field names that were illegally modified.\\n272\\t    pub fn check_immutable_fields(\\n273\\t        \u0026self,\\n274\\t        old_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n275\\t        new_data: \u0026serde_json::Value,\\n276\\t    ) -\u003e Vec\u003cString\u003e {\\n277\\t        let mut violations = Vec::new();\\n278\\t        for field in \u0026self.immutable_fields {\\n279\\t            let old_val = old_data.get(field);\\n280\\t            let new_val = new_data.get(field);\\n281\\t            // If the field existed before and is now different or missing, it's a violation.\\n282\\t            if old_val.is_some() \u0026\u0026 old_val != new_val {\\n283\\t                violations.push(field.clone());\\n284\\t            }\\n285\\t        }\\n286\\t        violations\\n287\\t    }\\n288\\t}\\n289\\t\\n290\\t// ── Database-scoped access control (US-038, FEAT-014) ────────────────────────\\n291\\t\\n292\\t/// A grant that scopes a role to a specific database.\\n293\\t///\\n294\\t/// Without a grant, users cannot access a database (unless they have a\\n295\\t/// wildcard `*` grant or admin role).\\n296\\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n postgres_test_guard() -\u003e MutexGuard\u003c'static, ()\u003e {\\n        static GUARD: OnceLock\u003cMutex\u003c()\u003e\u003e = OnceLock::new();\\n        GUARD\\n            .get_or_init(||
